content: DSM-5-TR general PD criteria: enduring pattern of inner experience/behavior deviating from cultural expectations in ≥2 areas (cognition, affectivity, interpersonal functioning, impulse control); inflexible/pervasive across situations; leads to clinically significant distress/impairment; stable over time (onset adolescence/early adulthood); not better explained by another mental disorder, substance effects, or medical condition. Specific criteria vary by PD type (e.g., Borderline requires ≥5 of 9 criteria including abandonment fears, unstable relationships, identity disturbance, impulsivity, suicidality, affective instability, emptiness, anger, paranoia/dissociation). DSM-5 includes Alternative Model for Personality Disorders (Section III) using dimensional approach (impairment in personality functioning + pathological traits).
